title: AdvPrompter Fast Adaptive Adversarial Prompting for LLMs
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are vulnerable to jailbreaking attacks that lead to generation of inappropriate or harmful content. Manual redteaming requires a time-consuming search for adversarial prompts, whereas automatic adversarial prompt generation often leads to semantically meaningless attacks that do not scale well. In this paper, we present a novel method that uses another LLM, called AdvPrompter, to generate human-readable adversarial prompts in seconds. AdvPrompter, which is trained using an alternating optimization algorithm, generates suffixes that veil the input instruction without changing its meaning, such that the TargetLLM is lured to give a harmful response. Experimental results on popular open source TargetLLMs show highly competitive results on the AdvBench and Harm- Bench datasets, that also transfer to closed-source black-box LLMs. We also show that training on adversarial suffixes generated by AdvPrompter is a promising strategy for improving the robustness of LLMs to jailbreaking attacks. Our code is available at http://github.com/ facebookresearch/advprompter.
impact: This paper presents work whose goal is to advance the safety and robustness of practically deployed Large Language Models. The strong performance of our method, even in the transfer-attack setting, solidifies concerns about the safety of LLMs in production. We firmly believe that exposing such vulnerabilities is crucial to build safe and robust AI systems. That said, we understand that our method could be used by bad actors to jailbreak existing production LLMs. Therefore, following the principle of responsible open science, we will disclose our finding to all interested parties before official release. Additionally, we contribute to the existing defense mechanisms, by showing that our approach can be utilized to improve the robustness of LLMs via adversarial finetuning (see Section 4.3)

query_input.scenario: Deployment of LLM chatbots with safety alignment
query_input.issue: Automated jailbreak prompts induce harmful, unethical, or objectionable outputs.
hazard: Automated generation of human-readable adversarial prompts that bypass LLM safety guardrails.
exposure: End users interacting with aligned LLM chatbots

hipporag_result.vulnerability: Aligned LLMs possess brittle safety guardrails that fail against human-readable adversarial prompts, enabling circumvention of intent-based restrictions.
hipporag_result.impact: End users obtain harmful, unethical, or objectionable content, facilitating misuse and eroding trust in aligned language models.
hipporag_result.dose_response: Automated generation of human-readable adversarial prompts that bypass LLM safety guardrails, through end users interacting with aligned LLM chatbots, triggers brittle safety guardrails that fail against such prompts, leading to harmful, unethical, or objectionable content, facilitating misuse and eroding trust.

dose_response: Automated generation of adversarial prompts enables bypassing safety guardrails, which in deployment of aligned chatbots exposes end users, leading to harmful outputs that erode trust and cause psychological harm.
vulnerability: Over-reliance on safety alignment creates a single point of failure exploitable by adversarial prompts.
Lack of user education on adversarial prompt risks increases likelihood of successful jailbreak exploitation.
Absence of real-time output monitoring allows harmful content to reach users before moderation.
impact: Erosion of user trust in AI systems after exposure to harmful outputs.
Psychological harm to users receiving unethical or traumatizing content.
Reputational damage and legal liability for deploying organizations.
